LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-17-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GEORGE ELWELL TRUCKING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MATTER IS OFF CALENDAR. THE PARTIES FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CCP 430.41 BECAUSE THEY DID NOT MEET AND CONFER BY TELEPHONE, IN PERSON, OR VIDEOCONFERENCE. THE PARTIES ARE REQUIRED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY FEBRUARY 16, 2024. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. Court received an email on June 24, 2024 indicating that this matter has settled and notice of settlement will be filed. The Court modifeis the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GEORGE ELWELL TRUCKING, INC.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Request For Sanctions is off calender. Case settled.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-04-2024 Continued To Jun-25-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT GEORGE ELWELL TRUCKING, INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Request For Sanctions.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
STEVEN J. BRADY, ESQ., of BRADY LAW GROUP, (415) 459-7300, for Plaintiff.
Tristan A. Mullis, Esq., of WATERKOTTE MULLIS MORENO & GARLES, PC, (213) 789-5620, for Defendant.

Counsel for Plaintiff does not stipulate to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem.

The subject matter is discussed. At conclusion of discussion, DEFENDANT GEORGE ELWELL TRUCKING, INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Request For Sanctions is continued to Law & Motion on Jun-25-2024 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302. The Judge Pro Tem is to prepare and submit to the court a report and recommendation.

Judge Pro Tem: John-Paul Deol. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Apr-25-2024 Set For Trial On Apr-07-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Set One), And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-13-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-11-2024 Continued To Jun-04-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-14-2024 Continued To May-08-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-07-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. TRISTAN MULLIS, Esq. of Waterkotte Mullis Moreno & Garles, P.C. (tel: 213-789-5620) for Defendant.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GEORGE ELWELL TRUCKING, INC.'s DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is overruled. At a minimum, the targeted second cause of action pleads aiding and abetting a tort. (Id. at 3:13-4:2; IG Wireless, Inc. v. Yi (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 630, 653-54; see also Quelimane v. Stewart Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 38-39 [test is whether complaint states any valid claim, not whether it is the claim plaintiff intended].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GEORGE ELWELL TRUCKING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is off calendar. The parties failed to comply with CCP 430.41 because they did not meet and confer by telephone, in person, or videoconference. The parties are required to comply with the code. Defendant's response to the complaint is now due by February 16, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
